headline: Should Easing Supermajority Rules on Major Deals Require Action From Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) Investors?
summary: In early April 2026, Skyworks Solutions proposed amending its Delaware charter to remove long-standing 80% supermajority voting requirements on major transactions, shifting future approvals to a simple majority if stockholders consent at the May 13, 2026 annual meeting. This move would materially reshape shareholder rights by lowering the threshold for mergers, asset sales, and substantial security issuances, potentially altering how quickly large corporate actions can be approved. Next,...
